---
title: 'catalog run'
---

Compose and execute one or more catalog entries, writing data to disk or stdout.

A single entry runs it directly. Multiple entries compose source + transforms.
Unbound entries can have their input streamed in over Arrow IPC.

To persist a composed expression back to the catalog instead of executing it,
use [`catalog compose`](compose.qmd).

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog run <entries...> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`entries`** — One or more entry names or aliases. Multiple entries compose
  as `source + transforms...`.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-c`, `--code` | none | Inline Ibis expression applied to the composed `source`. |
| `-o`, `--output-path` | `/dev/null` | Path to write output. Use `-` for stdout. |
| `-f`, `--format` | `parquet` | Output format. One of `csv`, `json`, `parquet`, `arrow`. |
| `--limit` | unlimited | Maximum number of rows to output. |
| `-i`, `--instream` | stdin | File to read Arrow IPC record batches from (used for unbound entries). |
| `--fuse` / `--no-fuse` | `--fuse` | Enable catalog source fusion. |
| `--rename-params` | none | Rename a parameter on a specific entry. Format: `entry,old_name,new_name`. Repeatable. |
| `-p`, `--params` | none | Override expression parameters. Format: `key=value`. Repeatable. |

## Examples

Run a single entry directly:
```bash
xorq catalog run src -o - -f csv
```

Compose source + transform and execute:
```bash
xorq catalog run src trn -o - -f csv
```

Add inline transformation code:
```bash
xorq catalog run src trn \
    -c "source.filter(source.amount > 100)" \
    -o - -f csv
```

Pipe Arrow data into an unbound entry:
```bash
xorq catalog run src -o - -f arrow \
  | xorq catalog run trn -o - -f csv
```

With parameter overrides:
```bash
xorq catalog run pipeline -p threshold=0.5 -o results.parquet
```

## See also

- [`catalog compose`](compose.qmd) — persist a composed expression instead of executing it
- [`catalog run-cached`](run-cached.qmd) — same semantics, but wraps the result in a parquet cache
